# Test fixture corpus

Small .docx manuscripts exercising specific pipeline features. Keep each
fixture minimal (1-2 pages) and named for what it exercises:

- `zotero_cited.docx` — Zotero plugin citations (CSL JSON field codes;
  Mendeley shares the same fixture since its payload is the same CSL shape)
- `endnote_cited.docx` — EndNote `ADDIN EN.CITE <EndNote><Cite>` field
  citations: split-run, multi-item, PAGEREF-nested, and one whose embedded
  XML carries an extra HTML-entity encoding layer. Generated by
  `make_endnote_cited.py`.
- `wordnative_cited.docx` — Word's native References > Insert Citation
  citations: `customXml/item1.xml` `b:Source` entries keyed by `b:Tag`,
  cited via a `CITATION <Tag> \l <lcid>` field nested inside a `w:sdt`
  citation content control. Generated by `make_wordnative_cited.py`.
- `hand_cited.docx` — NO field codes: literal in-text markers (`[12]`,
  `[3-5,8]` range, superscript numerals, `(Smith et al., 2020)`) plus a typed,
  numbered 12-entry reference list after a "References" heading, for plain-text
  reconstruction (plan item 14). Entry 7 is intentionally unmatchable so the
  Crossref reconciliation flags it. Generated by `make_hand_cited.py`.
- `bracket_cited.docx` — NO field codes: a typed reference list prefixed with
  `[N]` instead of `N.` (`[1] Author, Title, ...`), including one entry typed
  with no space after the closing bracket (`[4]Author, ...`), plus in-text
  `[2]` / `[3,5]` / `[1-3]` markers. Regression fixture for the bracket-prefix
  reference-list parsing gap (segmentation used to leave every entry
  `ref_number=None` and leak the raw `[N]` into the reconstructed text/key).
  Generated by `make_bracket_cited.py`.
- `equations.docx` — Word equation editor (OMML) content, inline + display
- `figures.docx` — three embedded images, captioned three different ways
  (Word Caption style, plain "Figure N:" paragraph, abbreviated "Fig. N:"
  paragraph) to exercise both figure/caption association paths from plan
  item 9. Generated by `make_figures.py`.
- `unsupported.docx` — one planted construct per preflight detector: text
  box, tracked-change insertion, floating (anchored) image, SmartArt
  diagram, equation-as-image suspicion. Generated by `make_unsupported.py`.
- `clean.docx` — well-behaved manuscript stub (Title/Heading/Caption styles,
  plain body text, no unsupported constructs). Generated by `make_clean.py`.
- `tables.docx` — a clean 3-column table (header row, mixed numeric/text
  columns) and a pathological table (horizontal `gridSpan` merge in the
  header, vertical `vMerge` merge in the body), exercising plan item 17's
  table-normalization filter (`latextify.ingest.filters.normalize_tables`).
  Generated by `make_tables.py`.
- `supplement.docx` — a small Supplementary Information manuscript for plan
  item 21: two captioned figures, one OMML equation, and two Zotero
  field-code citations (one sharing a DOI with `zotero_cited.docx`'s first
  citation, to exercise cross-document dedup; one new). Generated by
  `make_supplement.py`.

Fixtures are created as part of plan item 1's follow-through and each
feature item adds what it needs. Never commit real unpublished manuscripts.

`.docx` fixtures are generated, not hand-edited: each has a committed
`make_<name>.py` script (python-docx, with raw OOXML injection via
`docx.oxml.parse_xml` for constructs python-docx can't author directly,
e.g. text boxes and tracked changes). Regenerate with
`uv run python tests/fixtures/make_<name>.py` after editing a script.
